title: allium flavum subsp. flavum
intro: Allium flavum subsp. flavum, also known as the yellow-flowering garlic or golden garlic, is a subspecies of wild garlic that belongs to the Allium genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and strong onion-like scent. The medicinal properties of Allium flavum subsp. flavum include its antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of Allium flavum subsp. flavum include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
